text:
Bring lume's documentation into agreement with what lume actually is now, AND make the documentation itself honest, navigable, and installable-from - so no reader (operator, adopter, future contributor, or an agent driving the loop) is misled by docs that still describe lume as an unbuilt plan or oversell what it is.

WHY NOW. Eight workstreams (build-lume -> package-as-claude-plugin) turned lume from a design sketch into a real, tested, dogfooded engine that now ships as an installable Claude Code plugin. But the canonical design docs under docs/ (vision.md, scope.md, constraints.md, questions.md, all dated 2026-06-09) were written before/at the start of that build and still read as a forward-looking v1 specification ('the v1 spine', 'in scope for v1', 'the first slice is...', 'load-bearing assumptions to validate'), with a priority sequence of things mostly now done. The success criteria they set - 'lume is used to build lume' and the lagging outcome 'good enough to point at another repo' - have been MET. The READMEs were refreshed in 0008 and are broadly current, but they make claims the docs do not back with evidence, and the doc set is not cross-linked or navigable as a whole.

WHAT THIS WORKSTREAM IS. Four strands:
(1) RECONCILE with reality. Audit every doc against built reality and decide, per document, whether it is (a) still-accurate intent to keep, (b) stale and to be updated to current state, or (c) a historical design record to be FRAMED as such (preserved, not silently rewritten) so pre-build assumptions are not mistaken for current claims. Reconcile validated-vs-open assumptions and met-vs-deferred goals against the evidence in the eight closed workstreams' decisions/retros.
(2) INSTALL FOR USERS. Provide clear, user-facing installation instructions: how a non-author installs and runs lume as a Claude Code plugin (the marketplace add + /plugin install path, the python3 requirement, the first command lume seed, the iteration loop). Make this easy to find and correct.
(3) OBSIDIAN VAULT. The repo should also function as an Obsidian vault: docs are cross-linked (wikilinks / markdown links) into one navigable graph with the root README.md as the root note from which every other doc is reachable. Opening the repo in Obsidian yields a connected doc graph, not orphans.
(4) ADVERSARIAL, HUMBLE README. The project README is its own adversary. It opens with what lume IS and, prominently, why you SHOULD NOT use it - the honest disqualifiers, limitations, and failure modes before any benefit. Honesty over grand claims. Every claim is either backed by concrete evidence (a test, a closed workstream, a retro, a measurement) or explicitly flagged as NOT yet evidenced (e.g. 'not verified in a live session', 'assumed, not measured'). Humility is the governing tone: no marketing, no unearned superlatives. This embodies lume's own non-negotiables (stay true to intent; ceremony buys its cost) and the 0008 lesson that an unevidenced 'it works' is a smell.
(5) ADOPTERS REGISTRY. Add an ADOPTERS.md at the repo root where anyone who adopts lume in their own project can register themselves (name/handle + a link to their project). Today the only adopter is lume itself, so this file STARTS near-empty and that is the point: it makes the 'zero external users' disqualifier visible and falsifiable rather than hidden - it is the honesty policy made into a living artifact, and the channel through which n>1 adoption first becomes observable. Structure it so it can later be read by lume itself to look up adopters and gather usage insights, i.e. a lightweight, consistently-formatted, parseable entry shape (not free prose).

WHAT THIS WORKSTREAM IS NOT. Not an engine change (no product code; deterministic, stdlib-only, behaviour-preserving - the 313 tests and live .lume/ stay green). Not a marketing polish pass (the opposite: it removes overselling). Not new feature docs for things that do not exist. Not re-opening settled design decisions - it records what was decided and what is true, it does not relitigate. Do not manufacture documentation debt; refresh what misleads, leave what still serves.

DONE-WHEN. (a) A reader of docs/ and the READMEs gets an accurate, consistent picture of lume as it is today: a packaged, dogfooded Claude Code plugin with a built, tested iteration loop; each docs/ file is current or explicitly marked historical with its status. (b) User-facing installation instructions exist, are correct, and are easy to find. (c) The repo opens as an Obsidian vault: docs form one cross-linked graph rooted at README.md, no orphan docs. (d) The project README leads with what lume is and why you should not use it, every claim is evidenced or flagged as unevidenced, and the tone is humble - no grand claims. (e) README set and docs/ are mutually consistent. (f) No engine code changed; tests stay green. (g) ADOPTERS.md exists at root, documents how to register adoption, honestly records lume itself as the sole current adopter, is part of the vault link graph, and is structured so it can later be machine-read.

DISCOVERY/PLANNING DECISIONS LEFT OPEN. Which docs to update-in-place vs preserve-as-historical-with-a-banner vs retire; whether the adversarial README is the repo-root README (the GitHub/vault-root face) with the plugin/ README staying install-focused, or both; exact link style for the vault (wikilinks vs relative markdown links) and whether to commit an .obsidian/ config or rely on convention; where install instructions live (root README, plugin README, a dedicated page) and how to avoid duplicating them out of sync; how the evidence-or-flagged rule is applied concretely (inline citations to tests/workstreams vs an evidence section); the ADOPTERS.md entry shape and how machine-readable to make it now vs later; how much is hand-written vs derived from the engine (verb catalog, schemas, retros); and where the line sits between reconcile/record and relitigate. Begin with a discovery iteration that inventories every doc, classifies it against built reality, maps the current vs desired link graph, and lists the concrete reconciliations + the honest disqualifiers the README must state; then a planning iteration that resolves these forks and slices execution.
title: Refresh the documentation: reconcile with reality, install guide, Obsidian vault, adversarial honest README
